free_text: Neutron scattering cross section measurements for 56Fe
Experiment performed using 7-MV CN Van
de Graaff accelerator at the University of Kentucky
Accelerator Laboratory.
Enriched iron sample with weight
of 18.178 g, diameter of 1.52 cm and height of 1.45 cm
Scattered neutrons were registered using a
neutron detector consisting of a C6D6 liquid
scintillator coupled to a photomultiplier tube.
The ion beam was pulsed at a repetition rate of
1.875 MHz and bunched to a width of about 1 ns with a
klystron buncher. Time of flight was used to
determine scattered neutron energy and discriminate
against prompt gamma rays.
The C6D6 liquid scintillator has pulse-shape
discrimination properties that allowed the separation
of neutron and unwanted gamma -ray events.
for neutron energies below 5 MeV
for neutron energies between 5 and 8 MeV
Accelerated ions impinged on a gas cell attached to
the end of the beam line that contained the tritium
or deuterium gas typically at 1 atm pressure. The
3-cm long gas cell assembly consisted of a tantalum
lined stainless steel cylinder with a 3.3-mu-m thick
molybdenum entrance foil window.
Data were corrected for selfabsorption and
multiple scattering
Uncertainty in corrections for multiple
scattering and finite size
Uncertainty in detector efficiency
Statistical uncertainty
Overall uncertainty obtained by combining
individual contributions in quadrature.
Compiled by S.H.
OS. Reference corrected
